Title: Permanent posts for all housemen by 2028 under MOH reforms
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Health Ministry (MOH) targets to offer permanent posts to all housemen immediately upon completing their training by 2028, said Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad.
In his social media posting today, he said the target was part of the ministry's ongoing reform agenda through the Inter-Ministerial Joint Task Force (IMJTF).
"By 2028, MOH targets to offer permanent posts to all housemen immediately upon completing their training. A true 'whole-of-government' approach at work!
"IMJTF is officially yielding results in tackling our perennial HR issues, through the ongoing reform agenda. We hear your concerns and we are actively closing the gaps.
"This year alone, 4,500 contract medical officers will be absorbed into permanent roles, alongside 800 new positions approved annually," he said.
For the record, Dzulkefly said there is no recruitment freeze even with the operating expenditure budget realignment.
"We are on track to fill more than 18,000 vacancies across all our service schemes in 2026.
"While we push for better working conditions to combat burnout, replacing our medical specialists remains a complex, long-term hurdle."
Dzulkefly said he had tasked the new deputy director-general of Health (Medical) to strictly focus on overhauling and resolving issues surrounding the production of local medical specialists.
​
"Whether it involves local Master's programmes or the Parallel Pathway, we must secure a sustainable, world-class training ecosystem for our healthcare professionals.
​
"We are fixing the system to ensure it truly dignifies, especially, our young doctors," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SD Property expands urban redevelopment play with RM160mil Unirazak deal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Property Bhd (SD Property) is acquiring Wisma Universiti Tun Abdul Razak (Unirazak) here from Permodalan Nasional Bhd (PNB) for RM160 million as part of its strategy to expand its presence in urban redevelopment projects.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, the property developer said its wholly-owned Sime Darby Property (KLGCC Resort) Sdn Bhd had accepted a binding letter of offer from PNB.
The property comprises a 15-storey office building with a basement car park on a 5,922.4 square meters freehold site along Jalan Tun Razak, about one kilometre from the Kuala Lumpur City Centre.
The building, which is about 47 years old, has a net lettable area of 110,669 square feet and is fully occupied.
Its principal tenant is Unirazak, which occupies about 84 per cent of the leased space.
"Subject to obtaining approvals of development plans, the group intends to redevelop and position the asset into a premium, mixed-use development, with an estimated gross development value of RM900 million.
"The proposed development is targeted to be launched in 2028 and is expected to be completed within five years from the launch date," SD Property said.
The company said the acquisition is expected to be fully funded in cash through a combination of internally generated funds and borrowings, although the financing mix has yet to be finalised.
SD Property group managing director and chief executive officer Datuk Seri Azmir Merican said the acquisition positions the group to deliver a landmark development befitting the site's prime location while strengthening its presence in the premium property segment.
"The site, which we intend to redevelop into a premium, integrated mixed-use development, leverages our experience and expertise in developing lifestyle-driven projects such as KLGCC Resort, where we have delivered signature developments such as Jendela Residences and The Ophera," he said.
The acquisition is a related-party transaction as PNB is connected to Amanah Saham Bumiputera, a major shareholder of SD Property.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman fined RM500 for assaulting Sultan of Perak
Article:
IPOH: A woman was fined RM500 by the magistrate's court here today, after pleading guilty to using criminal force against the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, during the state's National Day celebration last year.
Nurhaswani Afni Mohamad Zorki, 42, entered the plea after the charge was read before magistrate Norizan Ridzuan, who also ordered one month's imprisonment in default of payment of the fine.
The single mother of five was
charged
with using criminal force against Sultan Nazrin at 8.20am on Aug 31, 2025, in front of the Ipoh City Council building on Jalan Panglima Bukit Gantang.
The charge, framed under Section 352 of the Penal Code, carries a maximum sentence of three months' imprisonment, a fine of up to RM1,000, or both upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Nasrul Hadi Abdul Ghani prosecuted, while Datuk Naran Singh represented Nurhaswani on a pro bono basis.
According to the facts of the case, Nurhaswani climbed onto the royal dais during the Perak National Day celebration and rushed towards Sultan Nazrin while the Perak state anthem was being played.
Although protocol officers attempted to stop her, she managed to grab the Sultan, causing him to nearly lose his balance before security personnel restrained her.
Investigations found that she had deliberately dressed in black to resemble a security officer in order to gain access to the royal dais.
She was subsequently referred to Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta, where she was diagnosed with schizophrenia.
However, a psychiatric assessment later found her fit to stand trial and capable of entering a plea.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor campaign debate risks rhetoric over policy, group says
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor Writers' Association has urged political parties to set aside calls for a debate during the Johor election campaign.
Its head, Amiruddin Ali Hanafiah, said such a debate would likely be dominated by political rhetoric rather than meaningful policy discussions.
He said the association believed the short campaign period was not conducive to substantive debates, as candidates would be more inclined to trade political attacks and election promises than present practical and implementable solutions.
While acknowledging that caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi and PKR Simpang Renggam division chief Dr Maszlee Malik were both capable debaters, he said the timing was inappropriate.
Amiruddin said any debate should be held after the election, when discussions could focus on governance, policy implementation and issues affecting the people.
He said that if a debate were to take place during the campaign, it should involve candidates contesting the same constituency so local issues could take centre stage.
"Debates involving state leaders should be between individuals of equivalent standing, such as a menteri besar and a party chairman or a minister, noting that meaningful debates are better held outside election periods," Amiruddin said in a statement today.
Citing the 2008 debate between Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Datuk Seri Ahmad Shabery Cheek as an example of constructive policy discourse.
Anwar, then opposition leader, faced former information minister Ahmad Shabery in a nationally televised debate.
The session, held at Dewan Bahasa dan Pustaka in Kuala Lumpur, focused on fuel price hikes under the theme "Hari ini membentuk kerajaan, esok turun harga minyak" (form the government today, reduce oil prices tomorrow).
It was broadcast live across multiple media platforms, including TV3. Both sides debated subsidy rationalisation and claims of potential savings against the fiscal burden of maintaining fuel subsidies.
Amiruddin said the association believed debates involving serving executive leaders or policymakers were more likely to generate practical solutions, while campaign-period debates risked becoming platforms for political point-scoring and blame.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asdion shares to be suspended July 14, faces delisting
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Asdion Bhd's securities will be suspended from trading on July 14, with the company set to be delisted from Bursa Malaysia's ACE Market on July 16 after it failed to appoint a replacement sponsor for its Guidance Note 3 (GN3) regularisation plan following the resignation of Kenanga Investment Bank Bhd.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, it was stated that the software developer failed to appoint a replacement sponsor after Kenanga ceased acting as its principal adviser and sponsor, effective immediately. Additionally, the developer did not apply for an extension of time.
"Under Rule 8.04(5) of the ACE Market Listing Requirements, Asdion may avoid delisting only if it submits an appeal by July 13.
"If the company submits an appeal to Bursa Securities within the appeal timeframe, the de-listing will be deferred pending the appeal decision, although trading in the company's securities will remain suspended from July 14," said Bursa Malaysia.
The exchange said that upon the de-listing of Asdion, it will continue to exist but as an unlisted entity and may proceed with its operations and corporate restructuring.
"However, the shareholders will be holding shares which are no longer quoted and traded on Bursa Securities, while the interests and rights of the shareholders will remain safeguarded under the Companies Act 2016," it said.
Asdion was classified as a GN3 company in August 2024 after its external auditors highlighted material uncertainty over its ability to continue as a going concern.
On July 3, 2026, the company announced that Kenanga had decided to cease acting as its principal adviser and sponsor after reviewing the progress of its proposed regularisation plan.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Death of tahfiz student Wan Ahmad Faris classified as NFA
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) has announced that the death of tahfiz student Wan Ahmad Faris Wan Abdul Rahman, who was found dead and believed to have been murdered in 2013, has been classified as no further action.
Attorney-General Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar said a thorough investigation revealed no evidence linking any other individual to the deceased's death.
He said the A-GC had reviewed the investigation papers concerning the Kok Lanas case, which was investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code involving the death of a student from Maahad Tahfiz Sains Nurul Aman, Kok Lanas, Kelantan.
"Further investigations were conducted regarding the narrative presented by the family. Throughout the investigation, statements were recorded from 84 witnesses, and 45 DNA samples were analysed by the Chemistry Department.
"The results of the comprehensive investigation found no evidence that could link any other individual to the incident of the deceased's death," he said.
He said after reviewing all the evidence, there were no leads to link any individual to the commission of an offence.
"Therefore, be informed that this investigation paper has been classified as no further action," he said.
He said this in a notification letter dated July 3, which was received by the lawyer representing the family of Wan Ahmad today.
Dusuki said the A-GC also expressed its deepest sympathies and condolences to the family over the loss of the deceased.
In December 2013, Wan Ahmad, a Form Four student, was found hanging inside a dormitory toilet.
A forensic expert who conducted the post-mortem confirmed the case as sudden death with no links to criminal elements.
However, in June 2016, the Coroner's Court ruled that Wan Ahmad's death was caused by the actions of an unidentified individual or individuals.
In August 2025, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail confirmed that
police would reopen the investigation
into the tahfiz student's death.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM JULY 6, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia extended its gains on Monday to close higher at 1,683 points.
Plantation and healthcare stocks led the advance, with plantation counters buoyed by renewed interest.
Healthcare stocks also gained as investors sought their defensive earnings profile.
The ringgit weakened against the US dollar to close at 4.0780.
Meanwhile, both Bitcoin and Ethereum extended their losses, falling to RM256,392 and RM7,195, respectively.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hamas announces dissolution of Gaza governing body
Article:
GAZA CITY: Palestinian resistance movement Hamas announced Monday the dissolution of the body that has governed the Gaza Strip for nearly two decades, clearing the way for a technocratic committee to implement civilian rule.
The move marks a significant political shift by Hamas, which has run Gaza since its fighters seized control from rival Palestinian movement Fatah in 2007 after winning legislative elections the previous year.
Since a ceasefire took effect in Gaza last October between Hamas and Israel, the group has repeatedly said it is prepared to step aside from day-to-day governance, but the thorny issue of its disarmament remains unresolved.
"The head of the government's emergency committee Mohammed al-Farra has officially submitted his resignation," Ismail al-Thawabta, head of Hamas' government media office told AFP.
"He has also decided to dissolve the committee to facilitate the administrative and governmental transition to the National Committee for the Administration of Gaza (NCAG)."
The NCAG was created by the Board of Peace that US President Donald Trump established when he brokered the ceasefire between Hamas and Israel in October 2025.
"Hamas has taken a new step in that it will no longer be in charge of the Gaza Strip, in order to remove any pretexts for the occupation, which continues its agression and war of extermination," Hamas spokesman Hazem Qassem told AFP.
"We hope for the swift entry of the National Committee for the Administration of Gaza, and Hamas affirms its readiness to hand over governmental responsibilities to the commmittee to ensure its success."
A Hamas official earlier told AFP that the group had already informed other Palestinian factions about its decision at a recent meeting in Cairo.
"The factions welcomed Hamas's decision, describing it as a serious step towards enabling the National Committee to take up its governing role," the official said.
The dissolution of the Hamas body paves the way for the NCAG, headed by Palestinian technocrat Ali Shaath, to assume administrative responsibilities in the territory.
The NCAG has remained based outside Gaza for months, reportedly due to Israeli objections to its entry into the war-devastated territory.
Hamas and other Palestinian factions have held several rounds of talks in Cairo with mediators to narrow differences, particularly over the second phase of the Gaza ceasefire.
The first phase involved the release of the last Israeli hostages held by Hamas in exchange for Palestinians detained by Israel.
The transition to the second phase, which was to involve Hamas's disarmament and a gradual withdrawal of Israeli forces from Gaza, has been stalled for months.
Israeli forces have actually expanded their presence in the territory in recent months, taking control of nearly 70 per cent.
Meanwhile, Hamas is demanding the establishment of a Palestinian administration before it will consider handing over any part of its arsenal.
The question of Gaza's post-war governance remains one of the main sticking points in negotiations on implementing phase two.
Israel rejects any return of Hamas to power, but also rejects a direct takeover by the Ramallah-based Palestinian Authority at this stage.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit ends lower as investors return to US dollar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed lower against the US dollar on Monday as investors returned to the greenback following its recent pullback after a weaker-than-expected US June employment report.
Investors also remained cautious ahead of the release of the US Federal Reserve's June meeting minutes this week, said Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid.
At 6pm, the local currency eased to 4.0825/0875 versus the greenback from 4.0690/0735 at Friday's close.
He said other Asian currencies, like the ringgit, were also generally weaker against the US dollar.
"Today, the market will be observing the incoming data point. Tonight the ISM index for the US services sector will be published.
"Consensus estimate has pegged the index at 54.2 points for June, slightly lower compared with 54.5 in May," Mohd Afzanizam told Bernama.
Meanwhile, the ringgit was mostly softer against a basket of major currencies.
It weakened versus the euro to 4.6594/6651 from 4.6497/6612 at Friday's close and depreciated against the British pound to 5.4440/4507 from 5.4296/4430 at the end of last week.
However, it strengthened against the Japanese yen to 2.5148/5180 from 2.5214/5277 previously.
The local note was traded lower against regional currencies.
It fell versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1554/1595 from 3.1483/1563 at Friday's close and weakened vis-a-vis the Thai baht to 12.2403/2600 from 12.2384/2744 previously.
The ringgit also slid against the Indonesian rupiah to 226.8/227.2 from 226.2/226.8 last Friday and fell versus the Philippine peso to 6.64/6.65 from 6.61/6.63.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EU keen to expand strategic cooperation with Terengganu
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: The European Union (EU) is keen to expand cooperation with Terengganu in strategic sectors, including the green economy, digitalisation, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET), following discussions with the state leadership today.
EU Ambassador to Malaysia Rafael Daerr said the three-day visit by EU ambassadors and representatives to the state, beginning Sunday, aimed to better understand Terengganu's development agenda and identify new areas of collaboration.
"We came to Terengganu to better understand the state, where it is today, where it wants to go and where there are opportunities for us to strengthen our cooperation.
"Terengganu has shown us that we can work together more closely," he told reporters after a meeting with Terengganu Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar and members of the state executive council (EXCO) at Wisma Darul Iman here.
The delegation earlier had an audience with the Regent of Terengganu, Tengku Muhammad Ismail Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin, at Istana Syarqiyyah.
Daerr described Malaysia as one of the EU's key trading partners, with existing cooperation spanning sectors such as semiconductors, electronics and industrial equipment, and highlighted critical raw materials as another area with strong potential for collaboration.
He said Terengganu offered significant opportunities for European investment, noting that several European companies are already operating in the state and that more than 2,000 European companies have operations across Malaysia.
Besides the state's established petrochemical industry, Daerr said the EU saw strong potential for cooperation in the green economy and digitalisation, adding that European companies bring not only investment but also technology and expertise, particularly in green technology, energy efficiency, water management and sustainable resource management.
"When European companies invest in Malaysia, they become part of the socio-economic fabric of the state. Success means creating partnerships where both sides benefit," he said, citing German semiconductor manufacturer Infineon, which employs about 14,000 people in Malaysia, of whom 99 per cent are locals.
Meanwhile, Daerr said cooperation with Terengganu in AI, TVET, higher education and research formed part of efforts to strengthen long-term economic and people-to-people ties.
He said Europe has a diverse AI ecosystem comprising many innovative companies specialising in various fields, with several already operating in Malaysia.
He added that the EU advocates a human-centred approach to AI, including protecting personal rights, safeguarding personal data and ensuring the technology is used responsibly.
On TVET, Daerr said several European countries have established strong vocational education systems that combine practical workplace experience with formal education, providing a model for closer cooperation with Terengganu.
He said continuous investment in education and skills development is essential as AI continues to evolve rapidly, adding that lifelong learning would better prepare young people for future employment.
Daerr also highlighted education and people-to-people exchanges as key pillars of EU-Malaysia relations, citing programmes such as Erasmus+ and Erasmus Mundus, which promote student mobility, academic collaboration and joint research.
He said there are currently at least two research projects involving higher education institutions in Terengganu, namely Universiti Malaysia Terengganu (UMT), in collaboration with partner universities in Europe, particularly in the fields of aquaculture and biodiversity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Banting school stabbing: Focus on school security, mental health support
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government must take immediate, holistic action to address security gaps in schools and the rising wave of youth mental health issues, said Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.
He said he was concerned over a stabbing incident involving a student at a secondary school in Banting today, adding that was heartbreaking to read reports on the case.
"The government needs to take immediate and holistic action to address safety issues in schools, alongside the rising number of cases involving mental health," the Umno secretary-general said in a Facebook post.
The incident occurred at about 9.30am during recess when the victim was allegedly
stabbed
, shocking the school community.
A male student interviewed outside the school claimed the suspect had entered the canteen area from the direction of the surau before the incident.
He said chaos erupted immediately afterwards, with students fleeing for safety.
The Education Ministry is prioritising medical care and psychosocial support for the school community following the incident.
Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, who visited the scene earlier today, said police had taken all necessary security measures and detained the suspect.
"Our primary focus right now is ensuring the victim receives medical treatment. Concurrently, providing a
psychosocial support
system for the school community is the ministry's top priority," she said in a Facebook post.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mat Sabu to voters: Ignore Pas directives, they change with political convenience
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu has urged voters to disregard political directives or "fatwas" issued by Pas, claiming they change according to the party's political interests rather than consistent principles.
The Kota Raja member of Parliament said Pas had repeatedly altered its political stance over the decades, citing its past relationships with Umno, DAP and more recently Barisan Nasional.
"Even if Pas is strong, don't worry. Don't follow Pas' fatwas. They only apply at that particular time.
"Later, the fatwas change. Pas could cooperate with DAP, then split with DAP. It formed Muafakat Nasional with Umno, then left that too.
"Now they are telling Pas supporters to vote for BN," he said at a ceramah here today.
He was referring to Pas's directive calling on its members and
supporters to vote for Umno or BN candidates
in state constituencies where Perikatan Nasional is not contesting in the Johor polls. (hyperlink:
Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man was quoted as saying that he was confident party members understood the importance of ummah unity and that PN is the platform to achieve that goal.
He had said that the move was also aimed at preventing a split in the Malay vote and maximising the opposition's chances of winning.
Mohamad meanwhile also recalled that in the 1970s and 1980s, the Islamist party had declared Umno members "kafir" (infidels), leading to deep divisions among Malay Muslims.
He claimed Pas supporters at the time would refuse to consume meat slaughtered by Umno members, while political tensions had even escalated into violence during elections.
Mohamad argued that such shifting positions showed Pas directives were merely political tools intended to secure votes.
"These are temporary views aimed at winning elections. We remain committed to Islam as Rahmatan Lil Alamin — mercy for all."
He added that Amanah remained committed to inclusive politics, pointing to the party's decision to field non-Muslim candidate Sharen Teo in Permas.
Mohamad also said the federal government was prioritising Johor's development through projects such as improving cross-border travel to Singapore and expanding public transport infrastructure, arguing that closer cooperation between the state and federal governments would better benefit Johoreans.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BM GreenTech expands renewable portfolio with NVP acquisition
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: BM GreenTech Bhd's unit has bought a 100 per cent stake in Nefin V Power Sdn Bhd (NVP), a special purpose vehicle with rights to develop a 29.99 megawatt alternating current solar photovoltaic (PV) farm under the Corporate Green Power Programme.
The acquisition was undertaken through BM GreenTech's subsidiary Plus Xnergy Holding Sdn Bhd.
BM GreenTech said the acquisition will strengthen its renewable energy (RE) engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning business by adding an upstream revenue stream to complement its existing project-based operations.
"The purchase of NVP will be financed through internally generated funds and undertaken via two sale and purchase agreements (SPAs).
"The first SPA involves the acquisition of 900,000 ordinary shares from Nefin Energy (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd, representing 90 per cent of NVP's total issued and paid-up share capital, for RM3.24 million.
"The remaining 100,000 ordinary shares will be acquired from Nefinco (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd for RM360,000," it said in a statement.
BM GreenTech said the acquisition will be settled in two tranches, with 50 per cent payable upon the execution of the SPAs and the remaining balance upon completion of the acquisition.
Following the acquisition, Plus Xnergy will expand its role in the NVP project from serving solely as the engineering, procurement and construction contractor to becoming the long-term owner and operator of the utility-scale solar asset.
The company said NVP's solar plant is expected to generate an average of 60,000 megawatt-hours of renewable electricity annually.
This will prevent more than 1.1 million tonnes of carbon emissions over its 25-year operating life.
"This acquisition is a step forward in our growth strategy to capture opportunities that move us up the value chain and provide more visibility on revenue. Utility-scale RE is still at its nascent phase in Malaysia.
"By entering this field now, we become one of the early providers of RE to corporates and industries, enabling us to tap the growth upside," BM GreenTech group managing director Chia Lik Khai said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: WCT Holdings accepts RM926.2mil construction contract in Abu Dhabi
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: WCT Holdings Bhd's wholly-owned subsidiary has accepted a RM926.20 million construction sub-contract in Abu Dhabi, the United Arab Emirates, through a 50:50 joint venture (JV) with Construction General Contracting House Ltd (CGCH).
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, WCT said WCT International Bhd and UAE-incorporated CGCH, through their 50:50 yet-to-be-incorporated joint venture, accepted the sub-contract letter of award issued by CGCH.
The job scope involves jointly undertaking, executing and completing the construction works for the Yas Riva Residences.
The project, owned and developed by Aldar Development LLC, involves the construction and completion of six residential buildings, each comprising 11 levels, in Abu Dhabi.
"Following the acceptance of the sub-contract, WCT International Sdn Bhd and CGCH shall, as soon as reasonably practicable, either directly or through their respective wholly owned subsidiaries, incorporate the JV company," the filing said.
The sub-contract works are expected to commence in the third quarter of 2026 and be completed in about 40 months, or within 1,218 days from the commencement thereof, it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Enest IPO oversubscribed 1.94 times ahead of ACE Market listing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Enest Group Bhd's initial public offering (IPO) has been oversubscribed by 1.94 times, ahead of its listing on the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia.
The pure bird's nest supplier said 2,871 applications for 85.33 million new shares worth RM11.12 million were received from the Malaysian public.
"For the Bumiputera portion, a total of 1,474 applications for 29.8 million new shares were received, which represents an oversubscription rate of 1.05 times.  For the public portion, a total of 1,397 applications for 55.6 million new shares were received, which represented an oversubscription rate of 2.83 times," it said.
Enest said that the 8.72 million new shares made available for application by the eligible director, employees and persons who have contributed to the success of the group have also been fully subscribed.
Meanwhile, its 72.66 million new shares made available via private placement to Bumiputera investors approved by the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry have been fully placed out.
"The 20.87 million shares (comprising 5.81 million issue shares and 15.05 million offer shares) by way of private placement to selected investors have been fully placed out," it said.
M & A Securities Sdn Bhd is the adviser, sponsor, underwriter and placement agent for the IPO.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Daddy issues? Nato's Rutte sticks to charm to keep Trump on side
Article:
WHEN Nato chief Mark Rutte travelled to Washington ahead of this year's Ankara summit he had a special weapon to please Donald Trump: a gold-lettered presentation on what a great job the US president has done for the alliance.
The move, designed to highlight how Europe is ramping up defence spending under pressure from Trump, was the latest leg of Rutte's relentless charm offensive to keep the mercurial US leader on board through a rollercoaster of crises.
"I just wanted to tell this story because, ladies and gentlemen of the American media, this is important," Rutte told journalists in the Oval Office as Trump looked on approvingly.
"This is your president, but also the leader of the free world, taking the leadership role as is necessary."
Trump responded with warm words of his own for the man seated next to him.
"He's a great guy, a great leader, great secretary general," he said. "I think if anybody else were in that position, we wouldn't even be meeting today."
Some 18 months into his second term, Trump has had falling-outs with a string of European counterparts from Germany's Friedrich Merz to Italy's Giorgia Meloni.
But as leaders on the continent have increasingly started standing up to the US president – Rutte, picked for the role thanks to his reputation as a "Trump whisperer", has stuck to a strategy of flattery to keep him onside.
"Rutte is one of the last men standing in Europe who still has a relationship with Trump," former senior Nato official Jamie Shea, now with Chatham House think tank, told AFP.
"People around Rutte say, you know, he has to do this... because for Nato, at the moment, the stakes are so high."
A year ago at a summit in The Hague, the alliance head scored a major victory by convincing leaders to agree to Trump's demand to commit to five percent of GDP to defence-related spending.
In a headline-grabbing moment that Trump seized on with glee, Rutte at the event went so far as to liken him to a "daddy" over the way he handled Iran and Israel.
But if Nato officials hoped that success in The Hague meant they were out of the woods, they proved to be mistaken – and Rutte would need all his diplomatic nous to keep Nato on an even keel.
[[nid:1482014]]
Just a few months later Trump started insisting the United States needed the vast Arctic island of Greenland, an autonomous part of Nato member Denmark.
The demands – and previously unthinkable idea that the United States might actually seize an ally's territory – rocked Nato to its core.
Once again Rutte stepped in to talk Trump down, getting him to back off with a compromise that kicked the issue down the road.
But the crises didn't end there.
When European leaders failed to back his attack on Iran – restricting access to US bases on their territories – Trump lashed out yet again at a favourite target: Nato.
He questioned why he should protect Europe if it didn't stand with him, and even floated the possibility of pulling out of the alliance.
Once again Rutte stepped in – jetting to Washington to see Trump. This time his charm didn't work so well, with diplomats reporting a difficult meeting.
Nonetheless Trump toned down his threatening language.
Known for his indefatigable bonhomie and upbeat mood, Rutte remains much appreciated behind the scenes at Nato.
Officials say he lacks the ego of other top politicians and, as he isn't currently eyeing elected office, does whatever is needed to play nice for Trump.
"Rutte is very much cut out for the job and we're very lucky to have him," one European diplomat told AFP.
"He is doing what he can, although even that has its limits."
While there is hope that Rutte's tactics will pay off again in Ankara – among some there is a sense that it's just a sticking plaster solution.
"There's the law of diminishing returns," Shea said. "You get less and less every time."
Despite Rutte's efforts, the United States is inexorably stepping back from Nato and Europe under Trump.
As he focuses on the US leader, some warn that Rutte risks missing out on broader discussions over how European security will look in the future.
The Nato chief sparked ire by telling Europe it could "keep on dreaming" if it thought it could defend itself without American forces.
"He's resisting that entirely, but because he's resisting it, he's not part of that particular debate in terms of developing new ideas," Shea said.
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Omoda/Jaecoo expands retail footprint with new Selayang 3S entre
Article:
Omoda/Jaecco Malaysia has strengthened its nationwide retail and aftersales presence with the official opening of a new 3S (sales, service and spare parts) centre in Selayang, operated by Carro Digital Sdn Bhd.
Head of Omoda/Jaecoo Malaysia Chris Tan said the new outlet reflects the brand's confidence in the Malaysian market and its commitment to growing alongside trusted dealer partners.
"The opening of the Selayang outlet not only adds another location to our network, but also reinforces our commitment to delivering greater convenience and a seamless ownership experience for customers across the country," he said.
The new facility follows Carro Digital's appointment as an authorised Omoda/Jaecoo dealer in March 2026 and extends the brand's reach to customers in Selayang, Batu Caves, Templer, Rawang and surrounding areas.
Designed to meet rising demand, the fully integrated 3S centre features six service bays with the capacity to service up to 624 vehicles a month, offering customers sales, servicing and spare parts support under one roof.
The showroom showcases the brand's latest retail design concept and includes customer amenities such as complimentary Wi-Fi and a coffee lounge.
Customers can also access a full suite of ownership services, including vehicle test drives, financing assistance, trade-in evaluations, insurance support and authorised aftersales care.
Omoda/Jaecoo now operates 53 showrooms nationwide through its network of authorised dealer partners as it continues to strengthen customer accessibility and service standards across Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KLIA touts: RTD seizes 54 vehicles in two-week crackdown
Article:
SEPANG: The Road Transport Department (RTD) has seized 54 vehicles in a two-week special operation targeting illegal airport taxi touts, or ulat, operating at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminals 1 and 2.
Its director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said the operation, conducted from June 12 to June 26, was carried out on the instruction of the Transport Ministry following complaints of rampant illegal passenger transport services preying on arriving travellers.
He said 59 vehicles were inspected during the operation, with enforcement action taken against 54 of them.
"The seized vehicles comprised 27 private cars, two company-owned vehicles, nine taxis and 16 e-hailing vehicles," he said.
He said a total of 76 notices were also issued, including 70 to Malaysians and six to foreign nationals for offences under the Land Public Transport Act.
Aedy said enforcement officers conducted undercover operations by posing as tourists, including using foreign nationals, to identify and catch illegal operators targeting newly arrived passengers.
He said syndicates typically approached passengers at arrival halls and offered unlicensed transport services at exorbitant fares, well above the RM65 to RM80 normally charged by licensed taxis or limousines travelling to Kuala Lumpur.
In one case, he said, a tourist from China who had agreed to pay RM60 for a trip from KLIA Terminal 2 to Kuala Lumpur was allegedly forced to pay RM836 upon reaching the destination and was threatened when attempting to leave the vehicle.
"RTD will not compromise with anyone involved in these illegal activities because they not only violate the law but also tarnish Malaysia's image as a tourist destination, especially during Visit Malaysia Year 2026," he said.
He said all vehicles used to provide illegal public transport services without permits would be seized as evidence, with RTD also applying for forfeiture orders through the courts where permitted under the law.
Aedy said that since January, RTD has inspected 151 vehicles linked to illegal taxi activities, taken enforcement action against 131 vehicles and seized 133 vehicles. Twenty-one forfeiture proceedings have also been initiated this year.
He said the problem was most prevalent at KLIA Terminal 2 due to the large number of international passengers arriving on low-cost flights, many of whom were unfamiliar with Malaysia's licensed transport system.
Among those caught were foreigners, mainly Bangladeshis, who allegedly targeted passengers from their own country because they shared a common language and could more easily persuade them to use illegal transport services.
"Some e-hailing drivers were also found bypassing the official booking application by directly soliciting passengers inside the airport so they could charge higher fares instead of following the regulated fare structure.
"To strengthen enforcement, RTD will increase the number of officers stationed at KLIA and introduce round-the-clock three-shift operations, replacing the current deployment of only 17 officers covering both terminals from 7am to midnight," he said.
He said RTD was working closely with Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd to display public advisories throughout the airport reminding passengers to use only licensed transport services and avoid touts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 143 e-waste containers removed in Port Klang operation
Article:
KLANG: Authorities have removed 143 containers carrying about 3,060 tonnes of electronic waste from Malaysia under the first phase of an enforcement operation at Port Klang, a senior official said today.
Port Klang Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the containers were removed as part of efforts to curb the illegal importation of electronic waste.
"The operation forms part of an integrated effort by the Joint Task Force on Scheduled Waste at Port Klang to prevent Malaysia from becoming a dumping ground for illegal e-waste imports.
"The task force, led by MCBA Port Klang, comprises the Royal Malaysian Customs Department, the Department of Environment, SIRIM Berhad, the police and the Port Klang Authority," he said in a statement.
He said enforcement personnel had so far inspected 685 containers under the operation.
"The removal of the 143 containers was carried out in accordance with legal provisions to facilitate systematic inspections, investigations and enforcement action against illegal e-waste imports.
"The second phase of the operation will begin on July 8, involving the phased removal of another 531 containers," he said.
Nik Ezanee said the exercise would be carried out with the cooperation of all relevant agencies to ensure safety, legal compliance and environmental protection remained top priorities.
He said the task force had also proposed that the Royal Malaysian Customs Department blacklist 50 companies identified by the police's Anti-Money Laundering Unit as being linked to the importation of illegal e-waste.
He added that the task force had proposed suspending the licences of two shipping companies for allegedly failing to comply with directives to remove the containers within the stipulated timeframe.
Nik Ezanee said the operation reflected the task force's commitment to strengthening border controls and eradicating the illegal importation of electronic waste through coordinated enforcement.
He said MCBA would continue enhancing cooperation with enforcement agencies in intelligence gathering, inspections and enforcement to safeguard the country's borders, protect public health and preserve the environment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump goads Italy's Meloni again on eve of Nato summit
Article:
ROME: Italy's defence minister stressed the importance of good transatlantic relations on Monday after US President Donald Trump again goaded erstwhile ally Giorgia Meloni, the Italian prime minister.
Meloni last month accused Trump of fabricating a story about her after the US president told an Italian TV channel that she had "begged" him to take a photo with her at a G7 summit in France.
With the two leaders due to attend a Nato summit in Turkiye this week, Trump appeared to reignite the dispute when he posted on Truth Social a picture of Meloni looking up at him with the caption "RESTRAINING ORDER NEEDED."
The Italian prime minister has yet to respond.
"I did not have any reaction (to the post), the fundamental thing is to maintain relations with a key ally like the USA," Defence Minister Guido Crosetto told Italy's Sky TV.
"People come and go but relationships remain," he added.
Opposition politicians were less restrained.
"Trump is a despicable, cheap bully," Carlo Calenda, the leader of the small Azione party, wrote on X, expressing his support for Meloni.
Meloni was once a vocal supporter of Trump and was the only European leader to attend his inauguration in 2025.
However, she criticised him this year for lashing out at Pope Leo over his condemnation of the Iran conflict.
That in turn prompted a blunt rebuke from the US president, who accused her of lacking courage.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia ends higher lifted by banking, plantation stocks
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended higher on Monday, supported by renewed buying interest in banking and plantation counters.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 4.48 points, or 0.27 per cent, to 1,683.53 from Friday's close of 1,679.05.
The benchmark index opened 3.35 points higher at 1,682.40 and moved between 1,678.37 and 1,685.49 throughout the session.
The market breadth was negative, with losers outpacing gainers 620 to 403, while 545 counters were unchanged, 1,082 untraded, and 10 suspended.
Turnover decreased to 2.68 billion units valued at RM1.69 billion from 2.79 billion units valued at RM1.93 billion last Friday.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said domestic sentiment turned slightly more positive following the easing of geopolitical tensions and the decline in crude oil prices, which could help alleviate inflationary pressures and support the broader economic outlook.
"The recent rebound also suggests that bargain-hunting interest remains intact, particularly in fundamentally sound blue-chip stocks," he told Bernama.
Among the heavyweights, Maybank put on 14 sen to RM10.94, Public Bank and Tenaga Nasional gained two sen each to RM4.91 and RM14.30, respectively, CIMB Group rose one sen to RM7.61, IHH Healthcare jumped 18 sen to RM8.33, and SD Guthrie increased 11 sen to RM6.15.
On the most active list, HHRG ticked up one sen to nine sen, ECKEM Holdings gained 1.5 sen to 13.5 sen, Tanco perked up one sen to 18.5 sen, while Silver Ridge slipped 2.5 sen to 18.5 sen, and Zetrix AI slid three sen to 76 sen.
As for the top gainers, United Plantations garnered 38 sen to RM33.50, Petronas Dagangan added 28 sen to RM19.54, Bintulu Port soared 21 sen to RM5.56, Hong Leong Financial climbed 18 sen to RM18.68, and Hong Leong Industries increased 14 sen to RM18.82.
For top decliners, Malaysian Pacific Industries dipped RM1.96 to RM45.82, Batu Kawan declined 46 sen to RM21.18, Nestle shed 44 sen to RM90.76, Kelington Group lost 20 sen to RM7.80, and Panasonic Manufacturing erased 19 sen to RM5.70.
On the index board, the FBM Emas Index rose 15.71 points to 12,472.72 and the FBMT 100 Index gained 18.34 points to 12,301.64.
The FBM Emas Shariah Index decreased 4.61 points to 12,287.62, the FBM 70 Index declined 34.71 points to 17,805.64, and the FBM ACE Index eased 1.74 points to 4,892.87.
Sector-wise, the Financial Services Index advanced 102.45 points to 19,934.42 and the Plantation Index improved 38.25 points to 9,007.79, and the Energy Index notched up 0.89 of a point to 759.49, while the Industrial Products and Services Index slid 1.08 points to 181.36.
The Main Market volume narrowed to 1.06 billion units valued at RM1.36 billion compared with 1.32 billion units valued at RM1.65 billion last Friday.
Warrants turnover expanded to 1.04 billion units worth RM141.68 million versus 854.95 million units worth RM106.34 million previously.
The ACE Market volume dropped to 571.10 million units valued at RM193.68 million from 612.79 million units valued at RM170.56 million on Friday.
Consumer products and services counters accounted for 149.74 million shares traded on the Main Market, industrial products and services (204.98 million), construction (74.31 million), technology (181.66 million), financial services (33.72 million), property (214.51 million), plantation (14.78 million), real estate investment trusts (10.46 million), closed-end fund (99,200), energy (59.29 million), healthcare (32.10 million), telecommunications and media (15.82 million), transportation and logistics (45.42 million), utilities (32.81 million), and business trusts (172,500).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Permaju unit secures Chery's brand Lepas dealership in Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Permaju Industries Bhd's wholly-owned subsidiary Cergaz Autohaus Sdn Bhd has secured a dealer agreement to sell Lepas vehicles in Malaysia.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing, Permaju said Cergaz Autohaus had entered into an agreement with Chery Auto Malaysia Sdn Bhd to be a non-exclusive authorised dealer for the Lepas brand within an assigned market area.
Under the arrangement, Cergaz Autohaus is allowed to buy and resell Lepas vehicles, and identify itself as an authorised dealer.
The agreement runs from July 8, 2026 to June 30, 2028, with an option for renewal at Chery Malaysia's discretion.
Permaju said the dealer agreement enables the company through its unit to expand its automotive business by becoming an authorised dealer for Lepas vehicles in Malaysia.
"The appointment is in line with the group's strategy to strengthen its presence in the automotive sector by broadening its vehicle portfolio and enhancing its market competitiveness.
"The addition of the Lepas brand is expected to complement Cergaz Autohaus' existing automotive operations, diversify its product offerings and increase opportunities to generate revenue from vehicle sales, after-sales services and spare parts.
"The board believes that the appointment will further strengthen the group's long-term growth prospects and enhance shareholder value through the expansion of its dealership business," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Police, military personnel free to decide who they want to vote for
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan has rejected claims that there is any manipulation in determining the votes of police and military personnel in the 16th Johor election.
Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president, said that whoever holds the posts of Home Minister or Defence Minister does not have an advantage in securing early votes from police and military personnel.
"Supporters among the armed forces or police have their own views and will make their own decisions," he said after attending a tea and meet-the-people programme with voters in the Kempas constituency at Taman Anggerik here today.
Also present was BN candidate for Kempas, Datuk Ramlee Bohani.
Ahead of early voting tomorrow, Mohamad said he hopes that all police and military personnel in the state will join other Johoreans in maintaining BN's mandate to govern the state.
"I hope that those in Johor will stand together with other Johoreans as voters to maintain the BN government in this election.
"I urge them to give the same support to BN candidates in this election as it will help ensure political stability in the state," he said.
At the same time, based on early voting turnout in the 2022 Johor election, he expressed confidence that BN would receive strong support from police and military personnel.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Maybank IBG lifts Malaysia's 2026 GDP forecast to 4.9pct
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Maybank Investment Banking Group (Maybank IBG) has raised its 2026 gross domestic product (GDP) growth forecast to 4.9 per cent from 4.4 per cent previously.
This reflects the economy's resilient performance in the first half of the year despite elevated geopolitical uncertainties.
According to Maybank IBG, economic data for April and May suggest another quarter of growth exceeding five per cent.
It attributed the stronger outlook to solid manufacturing activity and firmer net exports, supported by the artificial intelligence (AI) driven technology upcycle and Malaysia's position as a net energy exporter benefiting from higher commodity prices.
"Outlook for the second half of 2026 remains healthy, especially amid the sustained momentum in investment approvals, particularly in technology, renewable energy, industrial real estate and infrastructure segments," it said.
Maybank IBG maintained its positive outlook on the domestic equity market, keeping its year-end target for the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI at 1,750.
The target is underpinned by an expected 7.5 per cent earnings growth, continued benefits from the investment upcycle, resilient spending on consumer essentials and steady foreign investor participation.
"The expansion of FBM KLCI to 50 constituents, from 30 currently, promises to reinvigorate broader sectoral picks while benefitting from the momentum of the MY Value Up initiative.
"As AI and data centre themes take centre stage, Maybank has upgraded its view on the technology sector, which is expected to benefit from these long-term structural growth drivers," it added.
The firm also raised its 2026 GDP growth forecast for the Asean-6 economies to 4.7 per cent from its previous estimate of 4.5 per cent in March, citing improving economic conditions.
Maybank IBG said about 200 institutional investors and prime brokerage clients, representing a combined US$23 trillion (RM93 trillion) in global assets under management, are attending its flagship Invest Asean conference in Singapore from July 7-8.
The event brings together investors with 54 companies, including a sovereign wealth fund, as well as key government agencies in Singapore.
Maybank IBG chief executive officer Michael Oh-Lau said the 13th edition of Invest Asean underscores Maybank's continued commitment to connecting investors with corporates while providing insights to help them navigate an increasingly complex global environment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Focus on PH ability to deliver manifesto promises, says Mat Sabu
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu has dismissed claims that the Pakatan Harapan Johor election manifesto is a "copy-paste" document.
He said the focus should not be on the document itself, but on PH's ability to deliver its promises if given the mandate to govern.
The Kota Raja member of parliament said any political coalition could produce an attractive manifesto, but the real test was whether it could implement its pledges.
"Anyone can write a manifesto. What matters is whether it can be implemented.
"Give us the opportunity to govern. If we are not in government, how can we implement it?" he told reporters during a campaign event at Kampung Parit Tengah, Rengit here today.
Mohamad, better known as Mat Sabu, also expressed confidence that PH would be able to form the Johor government after polling on July 11.
"We are confident that. Insya-Allah, on July 12 we can form the government."
The Agriculture and Food Security Minister was responding to former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin's claim that PH's manifesto was merely a "copy-paste" document, with many of its proposals already introduced or being
implemented by the Barisan Nasional (BN)
government.
On July 3, PH launched its
"Johor Untuk Semua" manifesto
as its offer to voters in conjunction with the 16th Johor state election.
Among its key pledges is the Johor Health Scheme, which provides healthcare coverage of up to RM100,000 per year for the B40 and M40 groups, as well as persons with disabilities, in addition to reviving the Kad Sihat Johor programme, targeting benefits for 500,000 people.
Meanwhile, Mohamad said PH's campaign had gained momentum as polling day approached, helped by volunteers and party workers from outside Johor.
"Alhamdulillah, as polling day draws nearer, especially with assistance from our friends outside Johor, we can see the campaign gaining momentum.
"I also see that more members of the Malay community are beginning to lean towards supporting Pakatan Harapan."
He added that the coalition had observed encouraging signs of growing support among Malay voters during the campaign.
The Johor election will be held on July 11, with early voting taking place tomorrow.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police bust RM3.25mil illegal World Cup betting transactions in Op Soga XI
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have uncovered RM3.25 million in illegal betting transactions under Op Soga XI, a special operation targeting illegal sports betting syndicates capitalising on the FIFA World Cup 2026.
The operation, launched by the Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department on June 11, will continue until July 19 and is being conducted in collaboration with several agencies, particularly the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), to monitor and curb gambling promotions on websites and social media platforms.
CID director Datuk M. Kumar said 554 suspects were arrested and 422 raids carried out nationwide.
"Those arrested comprised 508 men and 46 women, including 341 Malaysians and 213 foreigners believed to be involved in illegal betting and online gambling activities," he said in a statement today.
He said police also seized RM167,062.09 in cash during the nationwide operation.
Kumar said investigators also detected 263 online platforms, including websites, applications and social media accounts, used to facilitate football betting and promote gambling activities.
"Action will be taken with MCMC's assistance to block the websites and remove the offending online content.
"All suspects have been taken to the respective district police headquarters for further investigation under various provisions of the Betting Act 1953.
"Foreigners are also being investigated under the Immigration Act 1959/63," he said, adding that investigations under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 are also being pursued where applicable.
Kumar said police would continue intensifying enforcement efforts and strengthening strategic cooperation with relevant agencies to combat illegal gambling syndicates, while urging the public to channel information to assist authorities in eradicating such activities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fadhlina: Medical, psychosocial support top priority after school stabbing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Education Ministry is prioritising medical care and psychosocial support for the school community following a stabbing incident involving a secondary school student in Banting this morning.
Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, who visited the scene earlier today, said police had taken all necessary security measures and detained the suspect.
"Our primary focus right now is ensuring the victim receives medical treatment. Concurrently, providing a psychosocial support system for the school community is the ministry's top priority," she said in a Facebook post.
The incident occurred at about 9.30am during recess, when the
victim was allegedly stabbed,
shocking the school community.
A male student interviewed outside the school claimed the suspect had entered the canteen area from the direction of the surau before the incident.
He said chaos erupted immediately afterwards, with students fleeing for safety.
Fadhlina said she hoped the principal, teachers and students would be given patience and strength during this difficult period.
She also urged the public not to circulate unverified information about the case.
"I request that the public refrain from spreading fake news or fabricating rumours, as this case is currently under active police investigation.
"I would also like to record my appreciation to all the agencies involved. Insya-Allah, if there are any updates regarding this case that can be shared with the permission of the authorities, I will do so from time to time," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM calls for fair, swift resolution to Felda settlers' land, housing issues
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for a comprehensive and fair resolution to long-standing issues facing Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) settlers, particularly regarding land ownership and second-generation housing.
Anwar said any resolution must be swift and centred on the community's welfare.
"During today's meeting (with Felda representatives), I stressed that all long-standing issues need to be resolved comprehensively and fairly, particularly those involving second-generation housing and settlers' land ownership," he said in a statement issued after a meeting with Felda's top management at the Prime Minister's Office today.
He said every issue must be examined carefully, followed by a clear resolution plan to safeguard the future of settlers and their families.
"The Madani government remains committed to strengthening Felda's institutions, restoring confidence in its management, and ensuring that all efforts undertaken genuinely benefit settlers and their families," he said.
The meeting follows an earlier report that the prime minister had summoned
Felda's top leadership
to address the agency's persistent challenges.
Second-generation housing has long been a source of frustration for the community.
In a previous report by the New Straits Times, second-generation settlers in Perasu, Gua Musang, voiced their hopes for
homeownership
, saying they wished to finally own their own homes to avoid sharing quarters with their parents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas to decide Negri Sembilan seats after Johor polls, says Tuan Ibrahim
Article:
KOTA BARU: Pas will wait for the outcome of the Johor election before deciding which seats to contest in the Negri Sembilan polls, its deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said.
Tuan Ibrahim said discussions on seat allocations for the Negri Sembilan election had been put on hold for now.
"We are putting it on hold for now. We will wait for the outcome in Johor before deciding," he said.
The Kubang Kerian member of parliament said Pas Kelantan had been tasked with assisting the party's election machinery in Negri Sembilan during the campaign.
Commenting on DAP's claim that Perikatan Nasional (PN) was afraid to face Pakatan Harapan (PH) in the Johor election, Tuan Ibrahim, who is also PN deputy chairman, dismissed the allegation.
"In politics, everything is about strategy. Our strategy includes asking Pas supporters in Johor to back Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates in selected seats.
"They are worried about our strategy. They are afraid of losing the contest," he said.
Tuan Ibrahim said the response on the ground had been encouraging following the directive.
"The response on the ground has been very positive, especially among the local community.
"The reality is that Malay voters have responded positively," he said.
PN has called on its
supporters to vote for Umno in Johor
constituencies where the coalition is not fielding candidates.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GTA eyes Brunei expansion with Safran partnership
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Global Turbine Asia Sdn Bhd (GTA) is expanding its regional ambitions after signing a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Safran Helicopter Engines Asia to extend their long-standing partnership into Brunei.
The MoU establishes a framework for both parties to complete the qualification, approval and contractual processes required to support the proposed expansion of GTA's authorised distribution and customer support operations in Brunei.
Any future activities will be subject to the fulfilment of agreed conditions and the execution of definitive agreements.
The company said the collaboration marks a key milestone in its long-term strategy to strengthen its presence beyond Malaysia.
"As a trusted industrial partner of Safran Helicopter Engines, GTA continues to strengthen its capabilities and position itself to support customers across Southeast Asia through its aerospace maintenance, repair and overhaul services," it said.
GTA has served as a certified maintenance centre for Safran Helicopter Engines in Malaysia for the past 15 years, providing maintenance, repair and technical support while contributing to the development of the country's aerospace industry.
GTA executive chairman Datuk Nonee Ashirin Mohd Radzi said the proposed expansion reflects the company's aspiration to become a leading aviation support specialist in the region.
"We are proud to take the next step by expanding our collaboration beyond our home market.
"This reflects the company's ambition to become a specialised aviation support business with strong technical capabilities, while continuing to deliver the highest standards of support and service to our customers," she added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Pakatan’s Pasir Raja candidate courts youth and Felda settlers using hybrid approach
Article:
KOTA TINGGI, July 6 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Pasir Raja state constituency, Mohd Fakharuddin Moslim is using a so-called hybrid strategy to win over voters with just four days left before polling day for the Johor state election.
He said the hybrid approach combined aggressive face-to-face campaigning on the ground with dominance of narratives on social media platforms, a method that proved effective as his machinery successfully completed a 100 per cent tour across all localities in the state constituency.
Through the strategy, he said, they were not only able to physically approach traditional voter groups, but also successfully penetrated geographical boundaries to engage with young people and outstation voters through the use of social media platforms.
“It allowed us to simultaneously engage with almost all segments of society, including small traders, farmers, youths, and Felda settlers. In terms of physical coverage, our machinery had successfully traced the entire Pasir Raja state constituency, including relatively remote areas such as Sungai Redan.
“Since all localities have been visited, our focus in the remaining few days of this final sprint phase is to conduct a second round to strengthen voter confidence,” he told Bernama here today.
Meanwhile, Mohd Fakharuddin said he is aware that young people were the determining group in the current political landscape, and digital platforms were the best space to spark awareness to attract them to return home to vote.
“Our main focus now is to use this digital impact to mobilise outstation voters, especially the youth, to return. I want to ensure this message reaches their gadgets that every vote in Pasir Raja determines the future of this area,” he said.
He said that he had utilised his background as a Felda settler’s son and a second-generation resident from the area when going down to the ground to build a close relationship with voters.
“I am grateful that there is a spontaneous chemistry created on the ground, the acceptance of Felda residents, including the first generation, deeply touched my heart. They welcomed me warmly and some even invited me to sit down, chat, and drink together at stalls while I was campaigning,” he added.
Mohd Fakharuddin is facing a three-cornered contest in the Pasir Raja constituency, which has 29,818 registered voters, against BN candidate Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba and Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate Yuhanita Yunan.
To get the latest news on the 16th Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor. — Bernama

Title: Dewan Rakyat passes bill to strengthen MyCC powers to tackle cartels and monopolies
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — The Dewan Rakyat today passed the Competition Commission (Amendment) Bill 2026, aimed at strengthening the Malaysia Competition Commission (MyCC) and ensuring comprehensive enforcement of the country’s competition law framework.
The bill was passed by a majority voice vote after being debated by 12 members of Parliament from both the government and opposition blocs.
In his winding-up speech, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the bill, which contained 34 amendments, was important in strengthening a fair, transparent and dynamic market amid increasingly complex cartel activities.
He said Sections 4 and 10 of the existing Competition Act specifically provide for the regulation of price fixing, market sharing, production control, bid rigging, and the abuse of a dominant position or monopoly.
Through the latest amendments, he said MyCC’s powers to require the provision of information had been extended to include the implementation of market reviews.
“This measure is to strengthen MyCC’s role so that market reviews are conducted in a more comprehensive and holistic manner, while addressing difficulties previously faced in obtaining information from government agencies, enterprises and certain parties,” he said.
To ensure the smooth management of an increasingly extensive organisation, Armizan said the bill also introduced new provisions under Section 17A on the delegation of powers and functions.
He said that without clear provisions, the efficiency of MyCC’s daily operations and administration could be affected.
Earlier, government and opposition MPs who took part in the debate on the bill raised concerns over the proposed extension of MyCC officers’ powers to impose financial penalties, fearing it could lead to abuse of power.
Chong Zhemin (PH-Kampar), who supported the proposal for MyCC to be given powers to impose financial penalties and late payment charges, said its implementation should be guided by clear, transparent and consistent guidelines to prevent undue burden on micro, small and medium enterprises and small businesses.
According to him, the penalties imposed should be stringent enough to deter large companies from treating them as a cost of doing business.
“If the profits gained from breaking the law are much higher than the penalties imposed, then the law will not deter anyone.
“In imposing penalties, we also need to distinguish between large cartels that deliberately distort the market and small enterprises that may commit offences due to a lack of understanding,” he said.
Isnaraissah Munirah Majilis @ Fakharudy (Warisan-Kota Belud) suggested that the government establish a MyCC branch in Sabah to strengthen the enforcement of competition laws and expedite action on complaints related to cartels and monopolies in the Borneo region.
The same issue was also raised by Datuk Abdul Khalib Abdullah (PN-Rompin) and Datuk Andi Muhammad Suryady Bandy (BN-Kalabakan).
The Dewan Rakyat sitting resumes tomorrow. — Bernama

Title: Health minister aims for all housemen to get permanent posts by 2028
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 6 — The Health Ministry (MOH) targets to offer permanent posts to all housemen immediately upon completing their training by 2028, said Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad.
In his social media posting today, he said the target was part of the ministry’s ongoing reform agenda through the Inter-Ministerial Joint Task Force (IMJTF).
“By 2028, MOH targets to offer permanent posts to all housemen immediately upon completing their training. A true ‘whole-of-government’ approach at work!
“IMJTF is officially yielding results in tackling our perennial HR issues, through the ongoing reform agenda. We hear your concerns and we are actively closing the gaps. This year alone, 4,500 contract medical officers will be absorbed into permanent roles, alongside 800 new positions approved annually,” he said.
For the record, Dzulkefly said there is no recruitment freeze even with the operating expenditure budget realignment.
“We are on track to fill more than 18,000 vacancies across all our service schemes in 2026!
“While we push for better working conditions to combat burnout, replacing our medical specialists remains a complex, long-term hurdle,” he said.
Dzulkefly said he had tasked the new deputy director-general of Health (Medical) to strictly focus on overhauling and resolving issues surrounding the production of local medical specialists.
​”Whether it involves local Master’s programmes or the Parallel Pathway, we must secure a sustainable, world-class training ecosystem for our healthcare professionals.
​”We are fixing the system to ensure it truly dignifies, especially, our young doctors,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Banting school stabbing: Student stable after hospital treatment
Article:
SHAH ALAM, July 6 — A student injured in a stabbing incident at a secondary school in Banting, Kuala Langat, is in stable condition after receiving emergency treatment at Hospital Banting, the Selangor Health Department said today.
In a statement, the department said the student arrived at the hospital’s Emergency Department at 10.13am and was immediately attended to by the medical team.
“The victim was given emergency treatment without delay. The patient’s condition is currently stable and he or she remains under close observation while undergoing further assessment to ensure appropriate follow-up treatment,” the statement said.
The department said the case is being investigated by the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and urged the public to refrain from speculating or sharing unverified information, advising them instead to rely on official channels for accurate updates.
Hospital Banting said it remains committed to providing the best possible care to the patient but would not disclose further details on the victim’s condition out of respect for patient confidentiality and the family’s privacy.
“The hospital will continue to provide its full cooperation to the authorities throughout the investigation,” the statement added.
Earlier today, the Selangor Education Department confirmed that the suspect is in police custody and said counselling teachers had been mobilised to provide psychosocial support and intervention for students, teachers and staff affected by the incident.
The department also said its immediate priority was ensuring the injured student received the best possible care while supporting the victim’s family.
Kuala Langat police have confirmed the stabbing incident, while investigations into the case are ongoing. Authorities have repeatedly appealed to the public not to circulate unverified information that could interfere with the investigation.

Title: Storm alert for Selangor, KL, Johor, other states until 9PM
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit eight states and Kuala Lumpur until 9 pm tonight.
According to a warning issued by the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) this evening, the states affected are Perak (Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Mualim) and Kelantan (Jeli and Kuala Krai).
Similar conditions are also expected to hit Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Raub, Bentong and Rompin), Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat), Negeri Sembilan (Seremban and Jelebu) and Johor (Segamat, Kluang, Mersing, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru).
In Sarawak, the affected areas are Sri Aman, Sibu (Sibu and Selangau), Kapit (Song), Bintulu (Tatau), Miri (Miri and Marudi) and Limbang.
In Sabah, the bad weather is expected to affect the Interior (Tenom, Beaufort and Tambunan), West Coast (Ranau), Tawau, Sandakan (Telupid, Kinabatangan, Beluran and Sandakan) and Kudat (Kota Marudu). — Bernama

Title: Three arrested for robbing migrant worker while posing as police in Manjung
Article:
IPOH, July 6 — Police have busted a robbery syndicate that impersonated policemen with the arrest of three local men within hours of their alleged robbery of a Bangladeshi man in Manjung last Thursday.
Perak Police Chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the 45-year-old victim lodged a police report at 8.06 pm, claiming he had been robbed by two local men who identified themselves as policemen in an incident at Pantai Remis at about 4.30 pm the same day.
He said the victim was forced into a Nissan X-Trail sport utility vehicle (SUV) while being handcuffed before the suspects seized his bag and fled with RM8,000 in cash.
“The suspects had threatened to take the victim to the Pantai Remis Police Station for further inspection. However, the victim became suspicious that he was being robbed when the suspects did not head towards the police station but instead drove in another direction,” he told a press conference here, today.
He said the victim then grabbed the steering wheel, causing the vehicle to veer onto the road shoulder at Batu 12, Segari, near Lumut, before the two suspects fled with the victim’s cash after stopping a passing motorcyclist to escape from the scene.
Mohd Alwi said acting on information, a team from the Criminal Investigation Division of the Manjung District Police Headquarters along with policemen from Pantai Remis and Segari police stations, arrested three local men along Jalan Besar Gunung Tunggal in Changkat Keruing at about 8.30 pm on the same day.
He said police also seized a Nissan X-Trail, several mobile phones, ATM cards, cash, handcuffs, an auxiliary police authority card and various other items believed to be linked to the case.
“Checks revealed that all three suspects have previous criminal records involving robbery, theft, vehicle theft, causing hurt, criminal intimidation and drug-related offences. Preliminary investigations also found that two of the suspects admitted to their involvement in the robbery,” he said.
According to him, the three suspects have been remanded for seven days from July 3 to 9 to facilitate investigations under Section 395 of the Penal Code for gang robbery and Section 170 of the Penal Code for impersonating a public servant.
He said police have not ruled out the possibility that the syndicate was involved in several other robbery cases using the same modus operandi in other districts in Perak.
Members of the public with information on the case are urged to contact investigating officer Insp Munawarrah Ahmad at 011-16240391 or the Manjung District Police Headquarters Operations Room at 05-6899222 to assist investigations. — Bernama

Title: Woman fined RM500 after admitting using criminal force against Perak Sultan during state-level National Day event
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — A woman who admitted using criminal force against the Sultan of Perak during last year’s state-level National Day celebrations was fined RM500 by the Magistrates’ Court in Ipoh today.
According to
Sinar Harian
, Magistrate Norizan Riduan imposed the fine after Nurhaswani Afni Mohamad Zorki, 42, pleaded guilty to the charge.
The court also ordered her to serve one month’s imprisonment if she fails to pay the fine.
Nurhaswani, a single mother of five, was charged with using criminal force against Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah at about 8.20am on August 31, 2025, in front of the Ipoh City Council building on Jalan Panglima Bukit Gantang Wahab in the Kinta district.
She was charged under Section 352 of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum sentence of three months’ imprisonment, a fine of up to RM1,000, or both upon conviction.
The case was first brought before the same court on September 8, 2025, but no plea was recorded after the court ordered Nurhaswani to undergo a month-long psychiatric assessment and observation at Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta in Tanjung Rambutan under Section 342 of the Criminal Procedure Code.
Deputy public prosecutor Nasrul Hadi Abdul Ghani urged the court to impose a sentence reflecting the seriousness of the offence, noting that the incident took place on National Day and involved the state’s reigning monarch.
He said the punishment should serve as a reminder to the public not to commit similar acts.
In mitigation, defence counsel Datuk Naran Singh, who represented Nurhaswani on a pro bono basis, conveyed his client’s apology to the Sultan of Perak for her actions and appealed for a lenient sentence.
He said she had previously worked as a restaurant waitress earning about RM1,700 a month, but is now unemployed and supported by her children after her divorce.
“This is the accused’s first offence and she also suffers from schizophrenia, for which she has previously received treatment,” he said.
Naran Singh also urged the court to consider the 55 days she had already spent in custody, including periods of remand, investigation, detention and hospital observation.
The court subsequently imposed a RM500 fine and ordered that her bail be refunded.
Court documents stated that Nurhaswani, dressed in black, climbed onto the stage during the National Day celebration before suddenly running towards Sultan Nazrin and grabbing him, causing the Ruler to nearly lose his balance.
Security personnel immediately restrained her before a police report was lodged.
